collector/benthos/services/leaderelectio.purpose: Implements Kubernetes lease-based leader election for the Benthos collector, ensuring only one replica acts as the active leader at a time. Exposes leader state via Benthos service.Resources generic key so plugins can gate work behind IsLeader().

patterns.name: CLI flags declared as package-level constants
patterns.description: Every tunable is a package-level const string (e.g. leaderElectionEnabledFlag) used in both flag definitions and ctx.Bool/String/Duration reads — never use raw string literals in two places.

patterns.name: Config struct as the sole handoff between CLI and Service
patterns.description: CLIOptCustomRunFlags callback populates a local Config value; CLIOptOnConfigParse passes it to NewService. The service never reads cli.Context directly.

patterns.name: IsLeaderKey context-free state via service.Resources generic
patterns.description: Leader state is communicated to other plugins through res.SetGeneric(IsLeaderKey, bool) — not via channel or context value. Plugins check with IsLeader(res).

patterns.name: Start/Stop with mutex-guarded started flag
patterns.description: Service.Start stores cancel func and sets started=true under mu.Lock; Stop calls cancel and resets started=false. Prevents double-start and ensures clean teardown.
patterns.example: s.mu.Lock(); defer s.mu.Unlock(); if s.started { return fmt.Errorf(...) }

patterns.name: Infinite retry loop inside goroutine in Start
patterns.description: The goroutine re-creates LeaderElector after each le.Run returns (lease lost or error) and re-tries after LeaseRetryPeriod unless ctx is cancelled. New code that runs inside leader scope must not embed its own retry.
patterns.example: for { le, _ := leaderelection.NewLeaderElector(lec); le.Run(ctx); if ctx.Err() != nil { return }; time.After(LeaseRetryPeriod) }

patterns.name: klog routed through Benthos logger at construction time
patterns.description: NewService calls logging.SetupKlog(logger) immediately so all client-go internal logs flow through the Benthos structured logger. Every new service using client-go must do the same.

patterns.name: Default leaseHealthCheckTimeout derived from LeaseDuration when zero
patterns.description: If LeaseHealthCheckTimeout is 0, it is set to LeaseDuration * 1.5 before constructing HealthzAdaptor. New code must not pass 0 to NewLeaderHealthzAdaptor.

key_file_guides.role: Declares all CLI flag name constants and the leaderElectionCLIFlags slice passed to service.CLIOptCustomRunFlags. Only place flag names are defined.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new tunable requires a const here AND a matching field in Config in service.go — missing either silently drops the value.

key_file_guides.role: Contains Config, Service struct, NewService constructor, Start/Stop lifecycle, GetLeaderElectionCLIOpts wiring function, and IsLeader helper. All public API lives here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: IsLeader returns true when the key is absent (leader election disabled path) — do not change this semantic without updating every caller that relies on the no-leader-election=always-leader assumption.

anti_patterns: Reading cli.Context flags by raw string literals instead of the declared const in flags.go
Calling leaderelection.NewLeaderElector outside the retry loop in Start — the elector must be re-created on each lease cycle
Using context.Background() instead of the ctx passed into Start/GetLeaderElectionCLIOpts — breaks cancellation on shutdown
Skipping logging.SetupKlog when adding another client-go-based service — klog will bypass the structured logger
Checking leader state by reading IsLeaderKey directly via res.GetGeneric instead of using IsLeader(res) — misses the absent-key=true default

decisions.decision: Leader state stored in service.Resources generic map rather than a dedicated channel or atomic
decisions.rationale: Benthos plugins receive *service.Resources from the framework; using SetGeneric/GetGeneric avoids threading a custom context or global variable through every plugin constructor.
Ensures the Kubernetes lease is actively released when the context is cancelled (graceful shutdown), preventing other replicas from waiting out the full LeaseDuration.

decisions.decision: Infinite retry loop re-creates LeaderElector per cycle
decisions.rationale: LeaderElector is not reusable after Run returns; re-creating it on each cycle is the pattern mandated by client-go to correctly resume after a lost lease.

code_examples.scenario: Wire leader election into a Benthos CLI application and gate a plugin on leadership
